title: Borojoa claviflora

Borojoa claviflora is classified as a species of plant in the family Malpighiaceae. 
It belongs to the genus Borojoa, which is a group of flowering plants native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. 
Borojoa claviflora is a climbing plant with small, yellow flowers and edible fruit, known for its medicinal and culinary uses in its native regions. 
This plant is also sometimes referred to as the "yellow dragon fruit" due to its vibrant yellow color and dragon fruit-like appearance.

intro: Borojoa claviflora, also known as the Borojo tree, is a species of flowering plant native to the tropical rainforests of South America, particularly in Colombia and Ecuador. The health benefits of Borojoa claviflora include its potential to support digestive health, lower blood pressure, and exhibit antioxidant properties, which may contribute to its traditional use in treating various health conditions.
